title: 10 Stocks That Could Benefit From the FIFA World Cup 2022
text: The FIFA 2022 World Cup is currently underway and is scheduled to run till December 18.

title: EA Puts Couples to the Test With the Better Together Experience for Launch of It Takes Two on Nintendo Switch
text: LONDON--( BUSINESS WIRE )--Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ: EA) – To celebrate the launch of the critically-acclaimed co-op adventure game It Takes Two on Nintendo Switch, EA invited couples to put their collaboration skills and feelings to the test. With 50% of all couples worrying about relationship boredom* and another 56% believing other couples have more fun, can gaming get the sparks flying again?

title: Can Videogame Makers Bounce Back From Their 2022 Lows?
text: All major videogame makers like Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), Sony Corporation (SONY) and Electronic Arts (EA) and Activision Blizzard (ATVI) are trying to bounce back from their recent lows.

title: Electronic Arts Stock Shows Once Again Why It Belongs in Your Portfolio
text: The game publisher put up another solid earnings result this quarter.

title: EA SPORTS™ FIFA World Cup 2022™ Update Available Worldwide
text: REDWOOD CITY, Calif.--( BUSINESS WIRE )--This week, Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ: EA) celebrated the arrival of its FIFA World Cup 2022™ game mode, available now as an update at no additional cost**. FIFA 23 fans can play as any of the 32 qualified nations in an authentic recreation of the full FIFA World Cup 2022™ from the opening match to the final game. Fans will be able to play a variety of game modes including FIFA World Cup™: Live, Your FIFA World Cup™, as well as take part in a variety of new FIFA Ultimate Team Campaigns.

Electronic Arts Needs FIFA to Stay Hot
text: Later World Cup could generate more interest in popular videogame franchise—and plug a hole in EA's holiday pipeline.

title: Marvel Signs A Three-Game Deal With Electronic Arts
text: Gaming publisher Electronic Arts and Marvel have agreed to make three games based on Marvel comics.

title: Electronic Arts' (EA) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Increase Y/Y
text: Electronic Arts' (EA) second-quarter fiscal 2023 results reflect strength in EA SPORTS portfolio and multi-platform live services business despite tepid net bookings.

title: Electronic Arts revises guidance following mixed second quarter
text: Video game publisher Electronic Arts Inc has revised its full-year guidance following Tuesday's second-quarter earnings call. Full-year net revenue is expected to range between US$7.55bn and US$7.75bn, revised down from US$7.6bn to US$7,8bn.

text: Electronic Arts, Inc. title: Electronic Arts (EA) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Electronic Arts (EA) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -5.19% and 1.52%, respectively, for the quarter ended September 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Electronic Arts stock dips as sales disappoint but earnings forecast increased
text: Electronic Arts Inc. shares dipped Tuesday, after the videogame publisher missed sales expectations for the quarter, as executives reduced their revenue and bookings guidance for the year but increased their forecast for profit.

title: EA lowers bookings expectation on strong dollar, gaming slowdown
text: Electronic Arts Inc lowered its annual bookings forecast on Tuesday as the publisher of "FIFA" and "Apex Legends" struggles with this year's surge in the U.S. dollar and a gaming industry slowdown from pandemic heights.
